meta.note: US federal actions that set or change what the United States charges on imports from Mexico. Each item is a primary government document; titles and abstracts are the government’s own words, unsummarized.

documents.title: Notice of Determinations and Request for Comments Concerning Actions in Section 301 Investigations of Acts, Policies, and Practices of Various Economies Related to the Failure To Impose and Effectively Enforce a Prohibition on the Importation of Goods Produced With Forced Labor
documents.agency: Trade Representative, Office of United States
documents.abstract: On March 12, 2026, the United States Trade Representative (Trade Representative) initiated 60 investigations related to the failure of various economies to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labor. documents.agency: Trade Representative, Office of United States
documents.abstract: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) provides for the establishment of a roster of individuals to serve on binational panels convened to review final determinations in antidumping or countervailing duty (AD/CVD) proceedings and amendments to AD/CVD statutes of a USMCA Party.
